concerns.analysis: The first page of the source PDF contains two highly meaningful organizational logos: the Great Seal of the State of North Carolina (top right) and the DEQ agency logo (bottom left). In the rendered HTML output, these images have been improperly converted into plain text paragraphs containing placeholder text instead of actual elements. Since the first page seals/logos are completely absent as valid images in the HTML structure, this is a missing image defect.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1 contains two prominent images: 'Great Seal of the State of North Carolina' at the top right and the 'DEQ North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality' logo at the bottom left.
concerns.output_quote:
The HTML output lacks elements for these logos, replacing them with plain text blocks:

[Image: Great Seal of the State of North Carolina]

and

[Image: DEQ North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality logo]

.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (2): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio; Daniel Blackman April 4, 2022 Page 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=2, pages_affected=2/4. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 1 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (1 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "... Carolina Department of Environmental Quality logo"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=1 (truncated=1, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 1 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=1. Examples: p3: "This is a completely white, blank image. There are". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.
